_README: Config for scripts/pseudolabel.py. ONE nnU-Net v2 model (Dataset803,
merged-label) downloaded from HuggingFace predicts BOTH spine and
pelvis (9 merged classes), so there are no separate region models.
OUT-OF-FOLD: pseudo-labelling reuses the SAME splits_5fold.json the
checkpoints were trained on. A token's held-out model is the fold whose
val set contains it (that fold never trained on it). pseudolabel.py runs
nnUNetv2_predict with ONLY that single fold. Tokens absent from every
val set (test-only / never trained) fall back to the 5-fold ensemble.
label_remap: model-output int -> canonical CTSpinoPelvic1K 10-class id.
5 -> 5 maps merged last_lumbar to L5; the L5/L6 split is a deferred
downstream refinement (count last_lumbar components sup->inf), NOT done
here. Change this mapping if you retrain with un-merged L5/L6.

checkpoints.notes: model class 5 = merged last_lumbar -> canonical 5 (L5); L5/L6 split deferred.
